archive_guidance: Store task-local feedback summaries in the active work-tracking FINDINGS.md, DECISIONS.md, HANDOFF.md, or reports directory.
Link accepted implementation feedback to an existing or new Taskmaster task before editing product or workflow surfaces.
Archive closed feedback with the work-tracking folder after the task PR merges.
Keep private memory and external chats out of the evidence chain unless summarized into repository artifacts.

domains.title: Stakeholder response context

intake_schema.purpose: Who or what produced the feedback, e.g. user, PR review, smoke test, CI run, session observation.
Specific behavior, gap, friction point, or request, quoted when possible.
Repository-native feedback category used by the routing matrix.
Manual severity label: blocker, high, medium, low, or informational.
Manual sentiment label: positive, neutral, concern, or blocker. This is human-reviewed, not machine-inferred.
Repository path containing supporting context, such as FINDINGS.md, HANDOFF.md, a report file, or a PR review link recorded in the tracker.
What should change if the feedback is accepted.
Existing or new Taskmaster task when the feedback requires scoped implementation.
Whether a human response is needed before closing the feedback item.

label: feedback-collection
manual_next_steps: Review the intake schema and routing matrix before collecting feedback.
Capture feedback as repository evidence tied to an active task.
Create or update Taskmaster tasks for accepted implementation feedback.
Use communication templates for any human-facing response draft.
Re-run this packet after major workflow, documentation, or stakeholder reporting changes.

manual_sentiment_labels.label: positive
manual_sentiment_labels.meaning: The feedback confirms a working behavior or useful outcome.

manual_sentiment_labels.label: neutral
manual_sentiment_labels.meaning: The feedback is informational or requests clarification without risk.

manual_sentiment_labels.label: concern
manual_sentiment_labels.meaning: The feedback identifies friction, uncertainty, or possible quality risk.

manual_sentiment_labels.label: blocker
manual_sentiment_labels.meaning: The feedback identifies a workflow, safety, or correctness issue that should halt related work.

metrics_checklist.review_question: How many feedback items were captured in repository evidence?
How many items still need a scoped task, response, or decision?
Which categories are generating the most friction or value?
Do accepted implementation items have Taskmaster task IDs?
Which items need human response before closeout?
Are closed items linked from work-tracking archive or final handoff evidence?

non_goals: No hosted form, survey platform, API endpoint, database, queue, dashboard, analytics service, or long-running feedback application is created.
No automatic sentiment analysis, NLP classification, scoring model, or external AI call is executed.
No owner assignment, notification, email, chat reply, webhook, ticket, or external response workflow is sent or created.
No Taskmaster task, documentation page, session, plan, work-tracking artifact, or Git state is mutated from feedback rows.
No external archive import/export, scraping, contact database update, PII processing, or retention policy automation is performed.
No source report, template, Taskmaster, session, plan, work-tracking, Git, or external state is mutated beyond requested feedback collection artifacts.
planning_guidance: Treat this packet as the review contract for capturing feedback as repository evidence, not as a live collection system.
Record actual feedback in the active task's FINDINGS.md, HANDOFF.md, DECISIONS.md, report files, or a follow-up Taskmaster task.
Use manual severity, sentiment, and routing review; do not infer intent or owner automatically.
Refresh supporting communication, onboarding, documentation, stakeholder, and Taskmaster evidence before claiming feedback readiness.
recommended_refresh_commands: python3 scripts/codex-task stakeholder report --report-file reports/stakeholder-reporting/latest.json --runbook-file reports/stakeholder-reporting/latest.md
python3 scripts/codex-task feedback collection-plan --report-file reports/feedback-collection/latest.json --runbook-file reports/feedback-collection/latest.md

response_workflow.definition: Feedback is recorded with source, observation, category, severity, sentiment, and evidence path.
A human has reviewed routing, severity, and whether a response or task is needed.
The feedback will be handled in the active task or a named follow-up task.
The feedback is valid but intentionally parked with rationale and revisit trigger.
The feedback will not be acted on and the reason is recorded.
The response/action/evidence is complete and linked from final handoff or archive.

routing_matrix.evidence_destination: active work-tracking FINDINGS.md and affected docs path
routing_matrix.response_guidance: Confirm whether the docs can be fixed inside the active task or need a follow-up task.
routing_matrix.route_to: FINDINGS.md or a documentation Taskmaster follow-up

routing_matrix.evidence_destination: session log, plan, tracker, and workflow evidence files
routing_matrix.response_guidance: Document the workflow decision before changing process files.
routing_matrix.route_to: HANDOFF.md, DECISIONS.md, or Taskmaster workflow follow-up

routing_matrix.evidence_destination: guard output, pytest evidence, and reproducer command
routing_matrix.response_guidance: Require a failing test or explicit non-enforceable limitation before implementation.
routing_matrix.route_to: guard test issue or scoped guard task

routing_matrix.evidence_destination: template path, registry path, and scanner output
routing_matrix.response_guidance: Keep the change portable and registry-first.

routing_matrix.evidence_destination: runtime contract, smoke-test report, MCP status, or agent-specific findings
routing_matrix.response_guidance: Separate Codex-owned and Claude-owned surfaces before mutating files.
routing_matrix.route_to: agent compatibility or runtime adapter follow-up

routing_matrix.evidence_destination: communication payload, stakeholder packet, or PR comment reference
routing_matrix.response_guidance: Draft response text only; do not send external messages from this command.
routing_matrix.route_to: communication template or stakeholder report update

routing_matrix.evidence_destination: Taskmaster task, enhancement packet, or DECISIONS.md
routing_matrix.response_guidance: Prioritize before implementation and record non-goals.
routing_matrix.route_to: Phase 5 enhancement review or new Taskmaster task
